label: Deadly drone strikes in Sudan and DRC highlight worsening regional humanitarian crises
label_zh: 蘇丹及剛果民主共和國的致命無人機襲擊，凸顯地區人道危機惡化

timeline.title: Lethal Drone Strikes in Sudan and DRC Claim Hundreds as Regional Conflicts Enter Dangerous New Phase
timeline.title_zh: 蘇丹和剛果民主共和國致命無人機襲擊造成數百人死亡，地區衝突進入危險新階段
timeline.sub_title: Recent aerial bombardments targeting markets and residential areas have killed over 200 civilians in Sudan and a UN aid worker in Goma, further destabilizing already fragile humanitarian corridors.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 近期針對市場和住宅區的空襲已在蘇丹造成 200 多名平民死亡，並導致一名聯合國援助工作人員在戈馬喪生，進一步破壞了本已脆弱的人道走廊。
timeline.summary:
The systematic use of increasingly sophisticated combat drones has reached a catastrophic threshold in both Sudan and the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) this week. United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights, Volker Türk, expressed profound alarm on Thursday, revealing that more than 200 civilians have been killed by drone attacks in Sudan since March 4 alone. This [ref:8, 6, 4, 1, 2] surge in aerial warfare marks a significant escalation in the ongoing conflict between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF), as both sides increasingly rely on foreign-supplied drone technology to bypass traditional frontlines and strike deep into populated civilian hubs.

In one of the most recent atrocities, a drone strike on Thursday targeted the Adikong market near the Sudan-Chad border, killing at least 11 people and injuring more than 20 others. According [ref:5] to Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF), which treated the wounded at a nearby hospital in Adre, Chad, the strike hit a fuel market, causing massive explosions and incinerating small-scale traders and their goods. This crossing serves as a critical lifeline for humanitarian aid and medicine entering the Darfur region; its disruption threatens to isolate millions of displaced persons already on the brink of famine. 

The political fallout in Sudan has been immediate and severe. On Saturday, Sudan’s army chief and Sovereign Council head, Abdel Fattah al-Burhan, visited the village of Shukairi in White Nile State to inspect the site of a March 11 strike that killed 17 civilians, including several schoolgirls. Standing amid the ruins of a secondary school and health clinic, Burhan ruled out any possibility of coexistence or negotiation with the RSF. He described the paramilitary group’s actions as devoid of humanity and vowed to continue the military campaign until the rebellion is entirely dismantled, effectively stalling regional and international peace initiatives.

Parallel to the crisis in Sudan, the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo has seen a similarly lethal escalation in Goma. On Wednesday, a drone strike hit the upmarket residential neighborhood of Himbi, killing Karine Buisset, a French aid worker for UNICEF. The attack, which [ref:3, 7] struck near the residence of former President Joseph Kabila, has prompted a diplomatic crisis, with the Congolese government and M23 rebels exchanging accusations of ceasefire violations. The strike occurred despite a fragile peace agreement recently brokered by international partners to enable regional stability and secure mining interests.

The humanitarian implications of these strikes are devastating. Across both regions, nearly 18 million people remain displaced, and the targeting of aid workers and vital infrastructure has made the delivery of relief nearly impossible in active combat zones. International leaders, including French President Emmanuel Macron and UN officials, have renewed their calls for all parties to respect international humanitarian law and halt the indiscriminate use of explosive weapons in urban areas. As drone warfare becomes the defining feature of these conflicts, the risk of a total regional collapse continues to grow, leaving millions of civilians caught in the crossfire of a technological arms race.
timeline.summary_zh:
本週，越來越複雜的戰鬥無人機系統性使用已在蘇丹和剛果民主共和國 (DRC) 達到了災難性的臨界點。聯合國人權事務高級專員沃爾克·特克週四表示深切憂慮，透露自 3 月 4 日以來，僅在蘇丹就有超過 200 名平民死於無人機襲擊。這次空中戰爭的激增標誌著蘇丹武裝部隊 (SAF) 和準軍事快速支援部隊 (RSF) 之間持續衝突的重大升級，雙方日益依賴外國供應的無人機技術來繞過傳統戰線，深入人口稠密的平民中心進行打擊。[ref:8, 6, 4, 1, 2]

在最近一次慘劇中，週四的一次無人機襲擊目標是蘇丹與乍得邊境附近的 Adikong 市場，造成至少 11 人死亡，20 多人受傷。據在乍得 Adre 附近醫院救治傷者的無國界醫生組織 (MSF) 稱，襲擊擊中了燃料市場，引發了大規模爆炸，並燒毀了小商販及其貨物。這個過境點是進入達爾富爾地區人道援助和藥品的重要生命線；其中斷威脅著數百萬本已瀕臨飢餓的流離失所者。[ref:5]

蘇丹的政治影響是直接而嚴重的。週六，蘇丹軍方總司令兼主權委員會主席阿卜杜勒·法塔赫·布爾漢訪問了白尼羅州 Shukairi 村，視察了 3 月 11 日造成 17 名平民（包括幾名女學生）死亡的襲擊現場。站在一所中學和一家診所的廢墟中，布爾漢排除了與 RSF 和平共存或談判的可能性。他將該準軍事組織的行為描述為沒有人性，並誓言將繼續軍事行動，直到叛亂被徹底剷除，這實際上使地區和國際和平倡議停滯不前。[ref:3, 7]

與蘇丹的危機平行，剛果民主共和國東部地區的戈馬也出現了類似致命的升級。週三，一次無人機襲擊擊中了戈馬的高檔住宅區 Himbi，造成聯合國兒童基金會的法國援助工作人員 Karine Buisset 死亡。這次襲擊發生在前總統約瑟夫·卡比拉的住所附近，引發了外交危機，剛果政府和 M23 叛軍互相指責違反停火協議。儘管國際夥伴最近促成了一項脆弱的和平協議以促進地區穩定和保護礦產資源，但襲擊仍舊發生了。[ref:null]

這些襲擊對人道主義的影響是毀滅性的。在兩個地區，近 1800 萬人仍然流離失所，援助工作人員和關鍵基礎設施的目標化使得在活躍戰區提供援助幾乎成為不可能。包括法國總統馬克龍和聯合國官員在內的國際領導人已重申他們呼籲所有各方遵守國際人道法，並停止在城市地區不分青紅皂白地使用爆炸性武器。隨著無人機戰爭成為這些衝突的決定性特徵，整個地區全面崩潰的風險持續增長，使數百萬平民陷入技術軍備競賽的槍林彈雨之中。[ref:null]

timeline.title: Drone Warfare Escalates Humanitarian Suffering as Strikes Kill Aid Workers and Civilians in Sudan and DRC
timeline.title_zh: 蘇丹與剛果民主共和國武裝無人機襲擊加劇人道災難，襲擊造成援助人員和平民死亡
timeline.sub_title: Devastating aerial attacks in Darfur and North Kivu target markets and residential areas, prompting international condemnation as the death toll from remote warfare surges across Central and East Africa.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 達爾富爾和北基伍的毀滅性空襲針對市場和住宅區，引發國際譴責，非洲中部和東部地區的遠程作戰死亡人數激增。
timeline.summary:
The escalating use of combat drones in Sudan and the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) has reached a lethal new threshold over the past 24 hours, causing mass civilian casualties and claiming the lives of humanitarian personnel. In Sudan’s West Darfur region, a devastating drone strike on Thursday targeted the Adikong border market near the Chad frontier. The [ref:5, 8, 6] attack, which hit fuel reserves, triggered massive explosions that killed at least 13 people and injured more than two dozen others. Witnesses and medical aid groups reported that the victims were primarily small-scale traders, including women and children, who were preparing for their daily work. This strike marks the second fatal aerial assault on the Adikong area in less than a month, highlighting the vulnerability of border regions that serve as lifelines for both trade and humanitarian aid.

The tragedy in Darfur follows a similarly horrific incident in Sudan’s White Nile state, where a drone strike on Wednesday hit a secondary school and a health clinic. That attack [ref:5] killed at least 17 people, many of whom were schoolgirls and a health worker. United Nations Human Rights Chief Volker Türk expressed profound alarm on Friday, noting that more than 200 civilians have been killed by drone strikes in Sudan since early March alone. Türk warned that both the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces are increasingly deploying powerful explosive weapons in densely populated areas, showing a reckless disregard for international humanitarian law.

Simultaneously, in the eastern DRC, the conflict has intensified with a drone strike on the city of Goma that has sent shockwaves through the international community. On Wednesday morning, a drone hit a residential building in the upmarket Himbi neighborhood, an area known to house many international aid workers. The strike killed three people, including a French national working for UNICEF. While the [ref:4, 7] M23 rebel group, which currently occupies Goma, blamed the Congolese military for the attack, the government in Kinshasa has stated it is investigating the origin of the explosions. The African Union and various UN agencies have condemned the strike, emphasizing that humanitarian workers and their facilities must never be targeted.

Beyond the drone strikes, violence continues to surge in the DRC's Ituri province. A brutal attack by the CODECO militia in Djugu territory killed at least 25 people, including seven children, between Wednesday and Thursday. Many of the [ref:1] victims were displaced persons who had recently returned to their villages to resume farming. This resurgence of militia violence, combined with the capture of key cities like Uvira by M23-aligned forces, has exacerbated one of the world's most protracted humanitarian emergencies.

The humanitarian toll of these twin crises is staggering. In Sudan, an estimated [ref:9] 33.7 million people now require urgent assistance, with over 12 million driven from their homes. In the DRC, internal [ref:2] displacement has surpassed 7 million people as peace efforts continue to falter. International [ref:3] observers warn that the introduction of advanced drone technology is fueling a new, more lethal phase of these conflicts, making it increasingly difficult for aid organizations to reach the millions of people caught in the crossfire.
timeline.summary_zh:
在過去 24 小時內，蘇丹和剛果民主共和國（DRC）戰鬥無人機的使用日益加劇，達到了一個致命的新閾值，造成大量平民傷亡，並導致人道主義援助人員喪生。在蘇丹西達爾富爾地區，周四的一次毀滅性無人機襲擊目標是乍得邊境附近的阿迪孔（Adikong）邊境市場。襲擊擊中了燃料儲備，引發了大規模爆炸，造成至少 13 人死亡，另有二十多人受傷。目擊者和醫療援助團體報告稱，受害者主要是小商販，包括婦女和兒童，他們當時正在為日常工作做準備。這是阿迪孔地區不到一個月內第二次發生致命的空中襲擊，凸顯了作為貿易和人道援助生命線的邊境地區的脆弱性。[ref:5, 8, 6]

達爾富爾的悲劇緊隨蘇丹白尼羅州一次類似的慘痛事件之後，當時一次無人機襲擊擊中了一所中學和一家診所。該襲擊造成至少 17 人死亡，其中許多是女學生和一名醫護人員。聯合國人權事務高級專員圖爾克（Volker Türk）於周五表示極度擔憂，指出僅在 3 月初以來，蘇丹已有超過 200 名平民死於無人機襲擊。圖爾克警告說，蘇丹武裝部隊和快速支援部隊越來越多地在人口稠密地區部署強大的爆炸性武器，顯示出對國際人道法極其魯莽的漠視。[ref:5]

同時，在剛果民主共和國東部，衝突加劇，一次無人機襲擊了戈馬市，震驚了國際社會。周三上午，一架無人機擊中了一個高檔社區 Himbi 的一棟住宅樓，該區域據悉是許多國際援助人員的住所。襲擊造成三人死亡，其中包括一名為聯合國兒童基金會（UNICEF）工作的法國國民。雖然目前佔領戈馬的 M23 叛亂組織將襲擊歸咎於剛果軍方，但金沙薩政府表示正在調查爆炸的來源。非洲聯盟和多個聯合國機構譴責了這次襲擊，強調人道主義工作人員及其設施絕不能成為目標。[ref:4, 7]

除了無人機襲擊外，剛果民主共和國的伊圖里省（Ituri）的暴力事件仍在持續激增。CODECO 民兵組織在 Djugu 領土的一次殘酷襲擊在周三至周四期間造成至少 25 人死亡，其中包括七名兒童。許多受害者是剛從流離失所者營地返回家園恢復耕作的居民。這次民兵暴力的捲土重來，加上 M23 相關部隊佔領了烏維拉（Uvira）等關鍵城市，加劇了世界上最長期的兩場人道主義危機之一。[ref:1]

這兩場危機造成的人道主義損失令人震驚。在蘇丹，估計有 3370 萬人現在需要緊急援助，超過 1200 萬人被迫離開家園。在剛果民主共和國，隨著和平努力持續失敗，國內流離失所者已超過 700 萬人。國際觀察家警告說，先進無人機技術的引入正在加劇這些衝突的新、更致命的階段，使得援助組織越來越難以接觸到身處交火中的數百萬人。[ref:9, 2, 3]

timeline.title: Drone Warfare Escalates in Sudan and DR Congo, Killing Scores of Civilians and Humanitarian Staff
timeline.title_zh: 蘇丹和剛果（DRC）致命無人機襲擊，造成數十名平民和一名聯合國援助人員死亡
timeline.sub_title: Indiscriminate drone strikes targeting markets, schools, and residential areas have claimed over 200 lives in Sudan since March 4, while a UN official was killed in eastern Congo.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 近期針對蘇丹葬禮車隊和剛果（DRC）戈馬市一住宅區的空襲，已造成包括女學生和一名法國聯合國兒童基金會工作人員在內的數十人死亡。
timeline.summary:
The humanitarian crises in Sudan and the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) have reached a devastating new threshold as the proliferation of drone technology fuels a surge in indiscriminate attacks. On Thursday, United Nations Human Rights Chief Volker Türk expressed profound alarm at the rapidly deteriorating situation, noting that advanced drone warfare is being used to deploy explosive weapons in densely populated areas with catastrophic results for non-combatants. Since [ref:2, 6] early March, the use of these remote-controlled platforms has transformed regional battlefields into lethal zones for civilians, school children, and aid workers alike.

In Sudan, the conflict between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces has entered an exceptionally bloody phase. Reports indicate that more than 200 civilians have been killed by drone strikes in just the last eight days. One [ref:7] of the most harrowing incidents occurred in Shukeiri village, located in White Nile state, where a drone strike on Wednesday hit a secondary school and a health clinic. The [ref:6, 2] attack resulted in the deaths of at least 17 people, the majority of whom were young schoolgirls. Further [ref:7, 3] west, in the Darfur region near the border with Chad, a Sudanese army drone strike targeted the Adikong market on Thursday. That [ref:7, 3, 5] strike killed 13 people, including small-scale traders and laborers, and set fire to critical fuel reserves. Medical teams from Doctors Without Borders reported treating dozens of wounded survivors, including many children under the age of 15, as hospitals struggle to cope with the influx of trauma cases.

Simultaneously, the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo has seen a similarly lethal escalation. In Goma, which has been under the control of M23 rebels since early 2025, a series of drone-linked explosions rocked the city center on Wednesday and Thursday. One strike directly hit a residential building used by humanitarian personnel, killing Karine Buisset, a French national working for UNICEF. The UN [ref:4] and the African Union have condemned the attack, which also claimed the lives of several other civilians. While [ref:1] the M23 rebel group has blamed the Congolese military for the strike, the government in Kinshasa has yet to take responsibility, stating only that an investigation is underway to determine the origin of the explosions.

The human cost of these technological advancements is compounded by a total breakdown in humanitarian access. In the DRC’s Ituri Province, the UN warns that displacement sites are becoming dangerously overcrowded, with the Plaine Savo site now hosting double its capacity from only two months ago. In Sudan [ref:6], the expansion of drone strikes into the White Nile and Kordofan regions has forced thousands more to flee, adding to the 11 million already displaced. Human rights officials and aid organizations are calling for an immediate cessation of the use of explosive weapons in populated areas, warning that the current trajectory of the conflict is leading toward an unparalleled regional emergency where nowhere is safe for those caught in the crossfire.
timeline.summary_zh:
蘇丹和剛果民主共和國（DRC）的無人機戰爭激增，在過去24小時內造成了重大的平民傷亡，這預示著地區衝突的危險升級。在蘇丹，至少有69人在兩起針對平民目標的無人機襲擊事件中喪生。其中最致命的一起發生在西科爾多凡，一架無人機襲擊了一輛載有參加葬禮人員的卡車。當地醫療消息證實，52人，其中大部分是同一家庭的婦女和兒童，在這場爆炸中喪生。隨後，在白尼羅河州，一架裝載爆炸物的無人機於週三襲擊了舒凱里村的一所中學和鄰近的健康診所。此次襲擊造成17人死亡，包括九名學生和多名教師，進一步摧毀了一個沒有軍事存在的社區。[ref:5, 1, 2, 7, 3]

同時，剛果民主共和國東部城市戈馬發生了一起備受國際譴責的知名無人機襲擊事件。週三凌晨，一架無人機襲擊了Himbi區的一棟住宅樓，造成至少三人死亡。死者中包括法籍人士、聯合國兒童基金會（UNICEF）員工Karine Buisset。此次襲擊發生在前總統約瑟夫·卡比拉的住所附近，並引發了剛果政府與M23叛軍之間激烈的相互指責。叛軍將此次襲擊歸咎於國民軍，而政府官員則一貫指責M23及其所謂的盧旺達支持者利用先進的無人機技術來破壞該地區的穩定。聯合國在剛果（DRC）的維和特派團警告說，針對國際人員的殺戮可能構成戰爭罪。[ref:9, 6]

兩國日益依賴遠程空中作戰，已從根本上改變了人道主義狀況。在蘇丹，快速支援部隊和正規軍被指控進行無差別砲擊，蘇丹醫生網絡報告稱，在科爾多凡和白尼羅河地區，無人機襲擊已成為幾乎每天發生的事件。來自空中的持續威脅迫使包括世界糧食計劃署在內的主要援助組織暫停或縮減在包括藍尼羅河州在內的幾個州的運營。人道主義工作者報告說，隨著辦公室關閉和工作人員疏散以躲避頻繁的轟炸，他們現在無法運送食物或醫療用品。

包括法國總統埃馬紐埃爾·馬克龍和歐盟官員在內的國際領導人，已發出緊急呼籲，要求所有交戰方遵守國際人道主義法。最近的死亡事件凸顯了非戰鬥人員和援助人員極易受到不斷演變的技術戰爭的傷害。由於在蘇丹和剛果（DRC）的政治解決努力仍陷入僵局，無人機使用的擴大預示著衝突進入了一個嚴峻的新階段，平民基礎設施——包括學校、醫院和市場——面臨被襲擊或遭受災難性附帶損害的風險越來越大。[ref:4, 10, 8]
